mcqs.question: A set of vectors {v₁, v₂, ..., vₖ} in a vector space is linearly independent if:

options.text: The only solution to a₁v₁ + a₂v₂ + ... + aₖvₖ = 0 is a₁ = a₂ = ... = aₖ = 0
At least one of the vectors can be written as a linear combination of the others
The vectors span the entire vector space
The set contains more vectors than the dimension of the space

mcqs.explanation: Linear independence means the only linear combination giving zero is the trivial one.

mcqs.question: A basis of a vector space V is a set of vectors that is:

options.text: Linearly independent and spans V
Linearly dependent and spans V
Linearly independent but does not span V
Any set of vectors in V

mcqs.explanation: A basis must both span the space and be linearly independent.

options.text: Linearly independent
Linearly dependent
A basis of R²
Spans R³

mcqs.question: The dimension of R³ (the space of all ordered triples of real numbers) is:

options.text: Infinite

mcqs.explanation: R³ has dimension 3 as any basis contains exactly 3 vectors.

mcqs.question: If V is a vector space with a basis containing n vectors, then:

options.text: The dimension of V is n and any set of n linearly independent vectors is a basis
The dimension is always greater than n
The dimension is always less than n
The dimension can be any number

mcqs.explanation: The dimension equals the number of vectors in any basis, and n independent vectors span V.

mcqs.question: The span of a set of vectors S = {v₁, v₂} in a vector space is:

options.text: The set of all linear combinations a₁v₁ + a₂v₂ where a₁, a₂ are scalars
Just the vectors v₁ and v₂ themselves
The set of all possible sums of v₁ and v₂
Always equal to the entire vector space

mcqs.explanation: Span includes all finite linear combinations of the given vectors.

mcqs.question: In R², what is the maximum number of linearly independent vectors possible?

options.text: Unlimited

mcqs.explanation: In R², dimension is 2, so at most 2 vectors can be linearly independent.

mcqs.question: If V is an n-dimensional vector space and W is a subspace of V, then:

options.text: dim(W) ≤ n and if dim(W) = n then W = V
dim(W) must equal n
dim(W) can be greater than n
W must have the same basis as V

mcqs.explanation: A subspace cannot have dimension greater than the whole space, and equality implies equality of sets.
